body: RAIL_SSEB20-220 (isolated body from clean re-pull, force='scene', 2 bodies: RAIL + BLOCK)

rail_overall_extents_mm.spec_table_L_range_for_H20: 100~1000 step (160) -- 220 is a valid configured length

mount_hole_pattern.x_origin_note: x=0 and x=180 are each G=20mm from the rail ends (rail spans x=-20..200 in the CAD local frame); 3 gaps of F=60mm between 4 holes; 20+180+20=220=L. Consistent.

hole_geometry.extraction_method_diameter_pitch: CAD-measured (vertex circle-fit on isolated RAIL body) AND manufacturer-confirmed (spec table 'Counterbored Hole d1xd2xh' column = 6x9.5x5.5 for H=20 row; F=60, G=20 in same row)
hole_geometry.thread_status: manufacturer-confirmed NOT tapped -- spec table lists rail hole only as 'Counterbored Hole d1xd2xh', with no M-thread callout; the M2x1.5/M3x3/M4x6 etc. values in the same table are the BLOCK carriage's S x l mounting-screw spec, a different column/feature, not the rail hole. Drawing callout is plain 'Od1' (no M-thread symbol). Per [[misumi-hole-type-codes]] this is consistent with Misumi linear-guide rails generally taking a clearance bolt from the top, secured into the T-slot nut below -- but the FASTENER SIZE for an Ø6mm clearance hole is inferred-standard (typically M5 or M6 socket-head cap screw with Ø6 clearance fits both); not manufacturer-named on this rail's own drawing/table.

cad_profile_through_thickness.interpretation: CAD step exactly reproduces table values: top radius 4.75 = Ø9.5/2 (counterbore); step occurs at y=5.5 = h (counterbore depth, measured from top face y=11 down to y=5.5); below the step radius drops to 3.0 = Ø6/2 (through-hole) down to the bottom face y=0.

cross_check_with_drawing.drawing_shows: H6-type rail cross-section with Od1/Od2/h counterbore profile, pitch F and edge margin G dimension call-outs, consistent with the measured/tabulated values.

extraction_method_summary: pitch/count/diameters/depth: manufacturer-confirmed (spec table) AND CAD-measured (independent vertex circle-fit) -- both agree exactly. Thread absence: manufacturer-confirmed (table column name + drawing plain-hole symbol). Fastener size/thread for the bolt that goes THROUGH this clearance hole into the extrusion T-nut: NOT specified by this rail's own documentation -- standard-derived/inferred only (must be resolved against the chosen T-slot nut, e.g. M5 or M6, by the lead).
